title: autobench.refactor v1
description: Per-case refactor-verifier result. Emitted after a tier-1 (symbol-rename) refactor candidate is checked by autobench.refactor_verifier.RenameVerifier — ast-grep scope, difftastic AST diff (or Python-AST fallback), and test-suite pass. The verifier scores continuous semantic-equivalence verdicts beyond the binary OK/CE/RE/WA family: RV (Refactor Verified), RD (Refactor Drift), RT (Refactor Test Fail). Source: /autobench/refactor.

session_id.description: Caller-supplied identifier grouping a batch of refactor verifications (e.g. an autobench run ID).

verdict.description: RV = Refactor Verified, RD = Refactor Drift (AST changes beyond rename), RT = Refactor Test Fail (test suite regressed).

refactor_type.description: Refactor class. v1 schema covers only the tier-1 symbol-rename case; future tiers (extract/inline, async migration, framework migration) extend the enum in v2+.

test_pass.description: Whether the project test suite passed under the post-refactor tree. null when no test command was configured.

ast_drift_files.description: Repo-relative file paths whose AST/structural diff exceeds the declared rename (the evidence behind an RD verdict). Empty for RV / RT.

details.description: Free-form evidence dict: ast-grep counts, difftastic JSON records, fallback-path indicators (tools_used), test runner output. Schema-free to allow tooling evolution without bumping the major version.
